evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useBottomOffset.ts` depends on pane/lightbox layout state (`usePanes`, `useLightboxOpen`), which ties it to a specific shell/layout subsystem despite being placed in a global `shared/hooks` package.
`src/shared/hooks/useCompletedTimestamp.ts` encodes generation-domain language (`generationProcessedAt`, "Completed ... ago") while living in `shared/hooks`, blurring domain ownership boundaries.

`tailwind.config.ts` is a large catch-all for global tokens plus highly specific effect families (`paper-plane-*`, `wes-*`, many bespoke keyframes/animations), indicating multiple styling concerns are flattened into one config surface.

Test placement conventions are mixed in the same area: hooks tests are grouped in `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/*`, while component tests are colocated as `src/shared/components/AuthGate.test.tsx` and `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.test.tsx`, making ownership and discovery less predictable.

Task creation modules repeat near-identical abstraction stacks (params interface -> validateX -> buildX -> createTask) in src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts, and src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts.

Multiple "normalization wrapper" helpers add indirection without real behavior changes (e.g., normalizeGenerationMappingInput in src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts and normalizeScopedGenerationInput in src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts).

Tiny timestamp hooks (useCompletedTimestamp/useProcessingTimestamp/useUpdatingTimestamp) are mostly formatter wrappers around the same base hook, increasing API surface for minimal semantic separation.

Result/error handoff contracts diverge across boundaries: `OperationResult` uses `{ ok, error: Error }` while edge utilities use `{ ok, policy, errorCode, message, recoverable }`, forcing adapters and mixed caller behavior.

Request parsing boundary in edge runtime exposes three different contracts (`parseJsonBody`, `parseJsonBodyStrict`, `parseJsonBodyLoose`) for one concern, with partially duplicated response construction.
Orchestrator linkage data is translated in multiple modules via legacy path probing (`orchestration_contract`, `orchestrator_details`, `originalParams...`) instead of a single boundary normalizer.
Invalidation orchestration has both deduped and non-deduped scheduling entry points, creating inconsistent lifecycle semantics depending on which API a caller chooses.

Very large orchestrator hooks/components with dense internal choreography reduce local clarity: src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts (525 lines), src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx (411 lines), and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx (503 lines).

Control flow is fragmented across many internal helper functions and adapter callbacks in src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts and src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx.

Low-level state logic and user-notification side effects are combined in src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts (polling policy, circuit-breaker counters, error classification, and toast emission in one class).

`GenerationRow.metadata` is typed as `GenerationMetadata | Record<string, unknown>` (`src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts:21`), and consumers repeatedly downcast it (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:305`, `:488`; `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:71`, `:90`).

`calculate-task-cost` coerces a DB row with `task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null` (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:227`) and then dereferences `typedTask.projects.user_id` without validating join shape/nullability (`:359`, `:428`, `:442`).

Edge JSON parsing advertises strict object output (`parseJsonBodyStrict`) but `parseJsonBody` always normalizes non-object JSON to `{}` via `asObject` (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:64-67`, `:97`, `:126-130`), weakening declared request-body guarantees.

findings.summary: Shared error utility has become a cross-layer hub with global mutable notifier state.
evidence: `handleError.ts` owns normalization, logging, notification payload shaping, and global notifier registration via `registeredNotifier`/`configureErrorNotifier`.
Low-level/shared hooks call `handleError(...)` directly (`useMobile.tsx`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`), coupling feature logic to one global error pipeline and notifier lifecycle.
findings.suggestion: Split error responsibilities into narrower modules (`normalize+log` core, optional UI notifier adapter) and inject notifier behavior at app composition boundaries so shared hooks depend on a smaller, non-global error contract.

findings.summary: Generation domain type is overloaded with legacy aliases and mixed persistence/view concerns.
evidence: `GenerationRow` includes many optional and overlapping fields across naming conventions (`createdAt` vs `created_at`, `thumbUrl` vs `thumbnail_url`, `shotImageEntryId` vs `shot_generation_id`) plus multiple concern types (DB identifiers, lineage, UI flags, task linkage).
`Shot.images?: GenerationRow[]` in `shot.ts` spreads this broad compatibility type into higher-level domain objects, making ownership boundaries less predictable.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit boundary-specific models (e.g., `GenerationRowDb`, `GenerationRowView`, `GenerationRowLegacy`) with single translation points, then narrow `Shot.images` to the UI/domain-ready shape.

findings.summary: Shared tooltip module mixes design-system primitives with feature behavior orchestration.
evidence: `ui/tooltip.tsx` defines base wrappers (`Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`) and also a specialized `TouchableTooltip` with touch toggling, outside-click handling, and hover-close logic.

Feature modules (`CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`) consume the same module as a primitive layer, so the UI foundation file simultaneously serves as component system core and behavior-heavy interaction implementation.
findings.suggestion: Keep `ui/tooltip.tsx` limited to primitive design-system wrappers and move `TouchableTooltip` into a separate feature/interaction module (or dedicated hook + wrapper) to preserve clear ownership and layering.

findings.summary: Task modules duplicate the same abstraction pipeline, creating high ceremony and drift-prone interfaces.
evidence: Each module defines separate params interfaces plus validate/build/create layers with overlapping concerns (required fields, seed/lora validation, orchestration metadata assembly).
Batch variants repeat the same structure (batch params + batch validator + single-task adapter) across modules instead of composing a shared contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-schema DSL (or typed builder) for common concerns (required fields, optional orchestration contract, seed/lora checks, batch expansion), then keep per-task modules focused on only truly unique fields/transformations.

findings.summary: Task producer/consumer seams rely on multiple key aliases for the same orchestration identity.
evidence: Sibling lookup in orchestrator completion ORs across multiple param paths for run/task id (`orchestrator_run_id`, `run_id`, nested `orchestrator_details`).
Task payload builders emit orchestration ids in mixed placements, while individual segment creation strips legacy keys before rebuilding payloads.
UI queries (useTasks) also depend on a specific legacy key (`params->orchestrator_task_id_ref`) for filtering.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical orchestration metadata contract (single location + key names), add a compatibility migration layer at ingestion, and remove multi-path lookups once backfilled.

findings.summary: Edit/lightbox internals are oversized orchestration units with high local cognitive load.
evidence: Files are 400-780 lines and combine environment discovery, state assembly, callback wiring, rendering decisions, and context object construction.
Large dependency arrays and broad model objects indicate many hidden coupling points and fragile refactor boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split by stable internal seams (environment resolution, mode state machine, render contract mapping) and enforce narrow per-layer input/output types to reduce cross-wiring.

findings.summary: Shot editor callback plumbing uses multi-step result/adaptor layers that obscure boundary behavior.
evidence: useShotImagesEditorCallbacks builds `runCallback` plus multiple `...Result` wrappers and then boolean adapters (`...Adapter`), creating parallel control paths for one operation.
Container and slot-mode hooks then consume similarly broad callback bundles, making it hard to trace error, return-value, and side-effect contracts end-to-end.
findings.suggestion: Collapse callback layers into a single typed command interface per user action (returning one discriminated result shape) and pass only required commands to each downstream consumer.

findings.summary: Cross-layer operation result shapes are inconsistent and force boundary glue code.
evidence: `OperationResult` models failure as `error: Error` only, while edge failures carry `policy`, `errorCode`, `message`, `recoverable`, and optional `cause`.
`generationTaskBridge` exposes both throwing and `OperationResult` paths for similar operations, while edge modules consistently return `EdgeOperationResult`.
`storage.ts` returns `EdgeOperationResult` for main flows but still has ad-hoc helper return shapes (e.g., plain `{ publicUrl?: string }`).
findings.suggestion: Define one shared cross-boundary result envelope (or a strict adapter layer) and require all public integration helpers to return that shape; keep throw-based variants as thin wrappers over the same canonical result.

findings.summary: Edge request body parsing exposes multiple incompatible APIs for the same seam.
evidence: `parseJsonBody` returns `EdgeOperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`, `parseJsonBodyLoose` returns raw object and silently defaults to `{}`, and `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `{ body?, errorResponse? }`.
`parseJsonBodyStrict` rebuilds HTTP response from the operation result message instead of reusing a single typed conversion path.
`ParseJsonBodyOptions.status` is threaded into strict wrapper response creation but is not part of core parse result semantics.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one parser contract (prefer `EdgeOperationResult`) and add a single utility that maps parse failures to `Response`; keep strict/loose behavior as mode flags rather than separate return shapes.

findings.summary: Orchestrator/task linkage translation is scattered across modules with legacy path probing.
evidence: `orchestrationContract.ts` defines a versioned canonical payload (`orchestration_contract.*`).
`billing.ts` still must probe multiple legacy paths (`orchestrator_task_id_ref`, `orchestrator_details.orchestrator_task_id`, `originalParams...`, direct `orchestrator_task_id`).
`generationTransformers.ts` also reads nested legacy/orchestrator payload variants (`originalParams`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`) for related metadata handoff.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single normalization module for orchestration metadata at boundary ingress, migrate writers to canonical contract fields only, and keep legacy fallback reads isolated to that module until backfill is complete.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation scheduling has inconsistent dedupe/cancellation semantics across entry points.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` keeps per-shot timeout refs and cancels prior delayed invalidations, but `scheduleGenerationsInvalidation` creates independent timers with no built-in dedupe registry.
The codebase also mixes direct query invalidation/cancellation helpers in `cacheUtils.ts`, leaving multiple ways to orchestrate near-identical cache lifecycle behavior.
Adjacent preload cleanup removes and invalidates nearby query data independently, increasing sensitivity to inconsistent invalidation timing across callers.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate invalidation scheduling behind one exported coordinator API that owns dedupe, delay, and cancel semantics; have cache utilities and feature callers depend on that single orchestration surface.

findings.summary: Timeline frame-spacing logic uses conflicting caps (50 vs documented/normalized 81), causing non-obvious behavior drift.
evidence: `MAX_FRAME_GAP` is defined as 81 in `timelineNormalization.ts:8`.
`calculateAverageSpacing` clamps with `DEFAULT_FRAME_SPACING` (50) in `timelinePositionCalculator.ts:16,48`, while nearby comment says max 81 (`:47`).
Fallback placement also uses `DEFAULT_FRAME_SPACING` (`:95`), so runtime behavior follows 50, not 81.
findings.suggestion: Unify on a single exported spacing cap constant and import it into both modules; align comments and clamp/fallback code to that constant so behavior and documentation match.

findings.summary: Video-enhance type surface advertises options that the payload builder silently drops.
evidence: `FilmInterpolationApiParams` includes `fps` and `video_write_mode` (`videoEnhance.ts:22-33`), but `buildVideoEnhancePayload` never forwards them (`:154-167`).

`FlashVsrUpscaleApiParams` includes `output_format`, `output_write_mode`, and `preserve_audio` (`:49-56`), but payload only forwards `upscale_factor`, `color_fix`, `output_quality`, optional `quality`, and `acceleration` (`:172-183`).
This creates a misleading local contract where callers can provide fields that have no effect.
findings.suggestion: Either wire all declared params into payload construction or remove unsupported fields from interfaces; add a narrow runtime validator that rejects unsupported keys to prevent silent no-op configuration.

findings.summary: Migration-era broad `unknown` boundaries remain in active shared types, weakening local precision and increasing silent edge-case handling.
evidence: `toJson` is a raw double-cast (`as unknown as Json`) with no shape guard (`supabaseTypeHelpers.ts:12`).
`GenerationMetadata` allows arbitrary keys via `[key: string]: unknown` (`generationMetadata.ts:47`).
Legacy migration input types merge modern types with `Partial<Legacy...>` and `Record<string, unknown>` (`legacyProjectImageSettings.ts:15,38-40`; `travelBetweenImages/types.ts:350-354`).
These patterns make malformed migration payloads harder to detect at construction boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Constrain migration surfaces with explicit decode/normalize functions that return strict canonical shapes, and confine broad unknown/index-signature types to isolated ingest modules only.

findings.summary: `shared/hooks` contains layout- and domain-specific hooks, weakening package ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useBottomOffset` reads pane lock/open state and lightbox visibility to compute shell positioning behavior.
`useCompletedTimestamp` is coupled to generation-task semantics via `generationProcessedAt` and completion phrasing.
findings.suggestion: Move these hooks into feature-owned packages (for example, pane/layout and generation/task presentation modules), then keep `shared/hooks` for truly cross-domain primitives.

findings.summary: Global Tailwind config acts as a monolithic container for unrelated styling concerns.
evidence: `tailwind.config.ts` contains extensive app-specific animation families and brand palettes alongside baseline theme primitives.

Root-level build/config files already hold multiple responsibilities (`vite.config.ts` includes runtime hosts, chunk policy, dependency dedupe), reinforcing top-level config flattening rather than bounded ownership.
findings.suggestion: Split styling concerns into imported modules (tokens, shared animations, feature/theme packs) and keep root config files as thin composition entry points.

findings.summary: Test file placement is inconsistent across adjacent shared packages, reducing discoverability.
evidence: Hooks tests are centralized under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/...`.
Component tests are colocated beside component files (`AuthGate.test.tsx`, `BatchDropZone.test.tsx`).
Within the reviewed shared area, no single directory-boundary rule is evident for where tests belong.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one explicit test placement convention per package type (all colocated or all `__tests__`) and apply it consistently in `shared/hooks` and `shared/components`.

findings.summary: Error notifier governance relies on a fragile runtime singleton rule that can crash on legitimate re-initialization paths.
evidence: README.md states notifier registration is done once at app startup.
handleError.ts throws when configureErrorNotifier receives a different notifier after initial registration.
This couples architecture correctness to function identity stability rather than an explicit lifecycle-safe contract.
findings.suggestion: Replace throw-on-second-registration with an idempotent lifecycle strategy (explicit reset API, replace-with-warning in DEV, or scoped provider ownership) and document allowed re-init behavior in README runtime contracts.

findings.summary: GenerationRow combines legacy and canonical schema variants in one global type, weakening architectural contracts and ownership boundaries.
evidence: generationRow.ts includes parallel alias fields across naming eras and semantics (camelCase + snake_case + legacy aliases) in one interface.
Comments in generationRow.ts indicate legacy compatibility concerns are handled inline on the shared domain type.
README.md runtime contracts do not define a formal normalization boundary for this compatibility surface.
findings.suggestion: Define a canonical GenerationRow contract and move legacy mapping to a dedicated boundary adapter layer; document this compatibility policy in README contracts so downstream modules depend only on canonical fields.

findings.summary: Core generation metadata type is too loose, forcing unsafe casts at call sites.
evidence: `GenerationRow.metadata` allows arbitrary records (`GenerationMetadata | Record<string, unknown>`), removing compile-time guarantees for known metadata keys.
Consumers recover expected fields by asserting `as Record<string, unknown>` and probing ad hoc (`useSegmentSlotMode.ts:305`, `:488`; `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:71-75`, `:90-95`).
This pattern makes metadata schema drift invisible to the compiler and shifts correctness to runtime conditionals.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a normalized metadata parser (runtime guard) that converts raw payloads to a stricter `NormalizedGenerationMetadata` type, then replace downstream `Record<string, unknown>` casts with that typed object.

findings.summary: Billing edge function bypasses row-shape validation with a double cast and assumes nested join presence.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` uses `const typedTask = task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null` (`index.ts:227`) instead of validating the query payload.
Code then assumes `typedTask.projects.user_id` exists in multiple places (`index.ts:359`, `:428`, `:442`, `:475`), which can throw if the relation is null/missing/unexpectedly shaped.
`complete_task/generation.ts` shows a safer pattern (`toGenerationTaskData`) that checks unknown input before use (`generation.ts:57-71`).
findings.suggestion: Replace the double cast with a narrow function (e.g., `toTaskWithProject`) that validates required scalars and `projects.user_id`, and handle missing relation explicitly before ledger/user queries.

findings.summary: Strict request parsing reports success for non-object JSON by coercing payloads to empty objects.
evidence: `asObject` returns `{}` for any non-object JSON value (`edgeRequest.ts:64-67`).
`parseJsonBody` wraps `JSON.parse(bodyText)` with `asObject(...)` and returns `edgeSuccess` even in strict mode (`edgeRequest.ts:97`).
`parseJsonBodyStrict` then returns this coerced body as a valid parsed result (`edgeRequest.ts:126-130`), which conflicts with a strict-contract expectation.
findings.suggestion: In strict mode, fail closed when parsed JSON is not a plain object (return `edgeFailure`), and reserve coercion-to-`{}` behavior for explicit loose mode only.
